title: Homework and the AI Question
modules: When AI Helps You Learn More
Spotting AI Slop in the Wild
synopsis: Follow a student who discovers AI can finish their homework in seconds — and then has to live with the consequences of every choice they make about how to use it. This course challenges learners to draw their own lines between smart assistance and dishonest shortcuts, leaving with a personal policy they actually believe in.
rationale: With AI homework tools now widely accessible, students urgently need a framework for ethical, effective use before habits form — and that framework must be built through their own reasoning, not handed to them as rules.
learning_outcome: Students can distinguish between AI use that deepens their learning and AI use that bypasses it, and can articulate and defend a personal policy for honest AI assistance in academic work.
source_signals: The AI-Education Death Spiral a.k.a. Let the Kids Cheat
26% of students ages 13-17 are using ChatGPT to help with homework, study finds
AI study buddy: how college students are using AI to learn smarter and faster
How AI study tools support students
His students suddenly started getting A's. Did a Google AI tool go too far?
